businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and Physical AI platform spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is expanding beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue reached $94.8 billion with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, the highest in two years. Key growth catalysts accelerate from April 2026 with Cybercab production and Optimus Gen 3 unveil.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 average (~23x) and auto sector (~16x). Reflects AI, robotics, and energy optionality but valuation remains contentious relative to current earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus. Growth expectations remain elevated but execution timeline extends into 2027+

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 reached a two-year high of 20.1%. Margin recovery signal amid ongoing pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving incremental growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. Total cash position of $44B provides buffer for capex ramp

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Conservative balance sheet. Among the lowest leverage ratios in large-cap tech

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP battery factory deal confirmed
growthDrivers.description: US government confirmed Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP battery manufacturing facility in Lansing, Michigan. Megapack 3 cells to be produced domestically starting 2027. Large-format 2.8L cells enabling 5 MWh per unit (28% increase from 3.9 MWh). Critical supply chain secured for energy business.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab 3/21 launch: logic + memory + packaging vertical integration
growthDrivers.description: Terafab is not just an AI chip factory but a facility integrating logic processing, memory storage, and advanced packaging in a single 2nm-class fab. Unprecedented vertical integration at $20B+ investment scale.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab production line targeting hundreds per week, April ramp
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds of units weekly, targeting 2M annual capacity. Fully autonomous vehicle without steering wheel or pedals. Regulatory approval remains a key hurdle but production infrastructure is on track.

growthDrivers.title: Macrohard (Digital Optimus): Tesla-xAI enterprise AI joint venture
growthDrivers.description: Musk unveiled Macrohard, a Tesla-xAI joint enterprise AI platform. Combined with Terafab, this completes the AI hardware + software vertical integration story.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb sales +35% YoY, global recovery signal
growthDrivers.description: CNBC (3/13): Tesla China-made EV sales reached 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. BYD numbers declined, signaling a reversal in China market dynamics.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share. FSD's 8.2 billion miles of driving data, Supercharger NACS standard adoption, and vertical integration form the core moat. However, BYD has overtaken Tesla in global EV sales, narrowing the competitive gap.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles driven. Overwhelming training data advantage vs competitors.
Proprietary battery (4680), custom chips (Terafab logic+memory), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS adopted as North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Supercharger network.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M in 2025 vs Tesla 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging tech

recentNews.headline: US government confirms Tesla-LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP battery supply deal
recentNews.significance: Dedicated Megapack 3 battery cell factory. Significantly strengthens energy business supply stability

recentNews.headline: Terafab expansion plans point to inevitable capital raise, first since 2020
recentNews.significance: 2026 capex guidance exceeds $20B. Secondary offering or debt financing likely

recentNews.headline: China Jan-Feb sales 127,728 units, up 35% YoY while BYD declines
recentNews.significance: China market reversal signal. Strengthens global delivery recovery expectations

recentNews.headline: Austin Cybercab/Optimus/FSD public pop-up event held
recentNews.significance: Public showcase of Cybercab and Optimus ahead of production ramp. Building consumer awareness

recentNews.headline: Cybercab production line targeting hundreds per week, preparing for ramp
recentNews.significance: April production start target. Regulatory approval for steering-wheel-free vehicle is key gate

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush maintains $600 high target (AI/robotaxi premium), GLJ Research at $25 low. 32-analyst consensus is Hold with deeply polarized views. Stocknear median $438.

risks.title: BYD competition accelerating, global EV share reversal
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M EVs in 2025 (+28%) vs Tesla's 1.64M (-8%). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging. Aggressive emerging market expansion in Brazil and Southeast Asia.

risks.title: Terafab execution risk: zero semiconductor experience
risks.description: Tesla has no semiconductor manufacturing track record yet plans to operate the world's largest 2nm fab. Logic+memory+packaging integration at this scale is unprecedented, even for TSMC or Samsung. Failure risks billions in sunk costs.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD investigation expansion, Cybercab regulatory hurdles
risks.description: 2.88M vehicles under FSD investigation, Austin robotaxi 14 traffic violations, railroad crossing incident. Cybercab requires regulatory approval for steering-wheel-free operation.

risks.title: Extreme valuation at 395x P/E
risks.description: TTM P/E 395x, Forward P/E 193x. Multiple compression risk if growth slows or competition intensifies.

risks.title: Terafab capital raise risk, potential dilution
risks.description: 2026 capex guidance exceeds $20B. Electrek flags first secondary offering since 2020 as likely. Shareholder dilution risk.

risks.title: Musk leadership dispersion + DOGE controversy
risks.description: Simultaneously running Tesla, xAI, SpaceX, X (Twitter), DOGE, and Macrohard. Focus dilution concern.

buyReasons.title: LG $4.3B battery deal + Terafab launch: energy + AI dual expansion
buyReasons.rationale: Megapack 3 domestic LFP cell supply secured, accelerating energy business growth. Terafab represents first step toward NVIDIA/TSMC independence. Both growth vectors materializing simultaneously.

buyReasons.title: Cybercab April production start + Macrohard AI market entry
buyReasons.rationale: First step toward robotaxi commercialization. Macrohard opens enterprise AI market. Revenue diversification beyond automotive.

buyReasons.title: China Jan-Feb sales +35%, energy business at 5-quarter revenue high
buyReasons.rationale: China rebound signals global delivery recovery. Energy segment gross profit hit $1.1B, reducing EV dependency.

buyReasons.title: Optimus robot + Fremont factory conversion
buyReasons.rationale: Model S/X discontinued, converting to Optimus production. Success unlocks $12T+ robotics TAM.

overallOpinion: The confirmed $4.3B LG Energy Solution battery factory deal secures Megapack 3 supply chain, removing a key bottleneck for energy business scaling. Lansing leaders are already projecting significant local economic impact from the $4B plant investment.

China sales rebounded 35% in Jan-Feb to 127,728 units while BYD volumes declined, a notable reversal in the world's largest EV market. Tesla is regaining pricing power amid the competitive landscape shift.

However, Terafab and broader capex plans exceeding $20B for 2026 raise the prospect of Tesla's first capital raise since 2020. If a secondary offering materializes, shareholder dilution is inevitable.

Technically, shares are recovering from the March 13 oversold bottom but remain below the 50-day SMA ($415) with a confirmed death cross. The 200-day SMA ($394) is providing near-term support. Catalysts are abundant but the capital raise overhang and stretched valuation warrant patience.

investmentVerdict.summary: Energy supply chain is secured and China sales are rebounding, but $20B+ capex plans and potential dilution from capital raising offset the positives. At 395x P/E with a death cross still active, the risk-reward favors waiting for clarity on the capital raise and 200-day SMA support confirmation.

threads.hook: Tesla locks in battery supply, China rebounds
threads.text:
$TSLA (3/18) $398.09 (+0.64%)

The LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP battery factory deal is confirmed. Domestic Megapack 3 cells from 2027. The biggest supply bottleneck for the energy business is now resolved.

China Jan-Feb sales hit 127,728 units, up 35% YoY, while BYD volumes dropped. Tesla is regaining momentum in the world's largest EV market.

But 2026 capex guidance exceeds $20B. A secondary offering is on the table for the first time since 2020. Testing 200-day SMA support. P/E 395x. Hold.

witty.hook: Tesla building so many factories it might need to sell stock
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/18) $398.09. Battery factory here, chip fab there, $20B+ capex everywhere. Tesla's wallet is starting to sweat. At least China sales are up 35%.

1/4
thread:
Megapack 3 supply is locked in with LG. Great news. But Electrek says a capital raise is coming, first since 2020. Shareholders, brace yourselves.

2/4

China beating BYD is a nice headline. P/E at 395x is less nice. This stock trades on dreams and delivers mixed signals.

3/4

Clinging to the 200-day SMA like a lifeline. 50-day is $17 away. Tesla wants to build everything itself, even shareholder anxiety. Hold.

4/4

empathy.hook: Tesla shareholders: good news with a side of worry
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/18) $398.09. Battery deal confirmed, China rebounding. Good news, but the capital raise talk makes it hard to celebrate fully.

1/4
thread:
The energy supply chain is genuinely secured now. When Megapack 3 scales, the revenue mix changes. This is a long-term positive worth holding onto.

2/4

Capital raising is growing pain, not distress. $20B+ in investment needs funding. What matters is where the money goes, not that it's needed.

3/4

200-day SMA is holding. Recovery from the 3/13 bottom shows resilience. No need to rush. Time is on your side here. Hold.

4/4

witty.hook: Tesla's capex bill might require printing more shares
witty.text:
$TSLA (3/18) $398.09 (+0.64%)

LG battery deal locked in, Terafab burning cash, $20B+ capex planned. Tesla is building factories faster than it can fund them.

Silver lining: China sales up 35%, BYD down. Megapack 3 supply secured. The growth story is alive, it just needs more money.

First potential stock sale since 2020. At 395x P/E, adding more shares to the float feels like adding water to expensive wine. Hold.

empathy.hook: Good catalysts, real concerns for Tesla holders
empathy.text:
$TSLA (3/18) $398.09 (+0.64%)

Battery deal confirmed, China rebounding. These are the catalysts you've been waiting for. The energy supply chain is finally secure and Megapack 3 can scale properly.

But the $20B+ capex reality means capital raising is likely. It's growth investment, not desperation, but dilution still stings.

The 200-day SMA is holding and the March 13 bottom showed buyers stepping in. Patient money has time on its side. Hold.
